sources_used: Amazon Q1 2026 earnings call transcript — April 29, 2026

current_multiples.p_fcf_note: FY2025 FCF of $7.7bn (CFO $139.5bn minus capex $131.8bn) yields a P/FCF of ~332x and is not a useful valuation anchor during the AI capex build cycle; prior-year FCF comps also unreliable

latest_results.operating_income_note: Record operating margin in company history; $23.9bn OI is a genuine operating figure — the $16.8bn Anthropic gain sits in non-operating income and does not affect segment OI
latest_results.eps_note: Reported EPS of $2.78 includes ~$16.8bn pre-tax Anthropic mark-to-market gain in non-operating income (ASC 321 treatment); after-tax impact at ~21% rate is approximately $1.23/share; clean operating EPS is approximately $1.55. This is a material one-time non-cash item.
latest_results.aws_growth_context: Fastest growth in 15 quarters; reaccelerated from 24% in Q4 2025; beat 25% consensus estimate
latest_results.advertising_beat: Beat $16.87bn consensus by 2.2%; trailing-12-month advertising revenue now exceeds $70bn
latest_results.aws_backlog_note: RPO of $364bn at March 31, 2026 excludes the subsequent Anthropic $100bn+ commitment; CEO Andy Jassy confirmed breadth across multiple customers, not 1-2 names
latest_results.guidance_q2_2026_notes: Both ranges materially above prior street consensus (~$189bn revenue, ~$17-18bn OI); assumes Prime Day in Q2 2026; includes ~10bp unfavorable FX; OI range reflects SBC seasonality, fuel inflation, and ~$1.0bn YoY Amazon Leo cost increase
latest_results.earnings_beat_summary: Beat on every line: revenue $181.5bn vs $177.3bn expected (+$4.2bn); operating income $23.9bn vs ~$20.7bn expected (+15%); EPS $2.78 vs $1.65 expected (though headline EPS is Anthropic-inflated). Genuine operating beat is in OI and AWS growth.

fy2026.eps_gaap_source_notes: SimplyWallSt revised down to $6.72 post-Q1 2026 (from $7.85 pre-earnings); TIKR cites $7.72 normalized; Amazon trades at fwd PE of 28.1x vs est $8.21 per FinBox; WallStreetZen consensus $9.00 with low $7.49 and high $10.43. Midpoint of credible operating estimates is $7.75.

fy2027.eps_gaap_source: JPMorgan post-Q1 2026 model; used 32x FY2027 EPS of $10.37 to derive $330 target

analyst_tape.key_thesis: AWS Q1 28% growth (15-quarter high) and record 13.1% operating margin validate the AI infrastructure thesis; reiterated bullish outlook May 15 after CEO shareholder letter
32x FY2027 GAAP EPS of $10.37; AWS reacceleration and retail margin expansion improve earnings trajectory; target reflects improved earnings quality, not just revenue
Cloud acceleration and retail margin expansion support 40%+ upside from pre-earnings levels; AWS demand backlog provides multi-year revenue visibility
Highest cluster target post-Q1; $364bn AWS backlog and $200bn capex commitment signal durable demand conversion runway
Largest proportional raise among post-Q1 cluster; AWS growth acceleration and advertising beat drove multiple re-rating
Beat on every line with EPS nearly doubling estimates; AWS and advertising dual-engine growth supports premium valuation
Post-earnings reaffirmation; AWS momentum and margin trajectory intact; ~30% upside from current price; reiterates entry case following ATH pullback

AWS 'losing its lead' — Azure grew 39% and GCP 48% vs AWS 24% in Q4 2025; no in-house frontier AI lab and no primary OpenAI partnership; retail threatened by chat-driven Internet. NOTE: This thesis was directly refuted by Q1 2026 AWS 28% growth reacceleration. Remains the sole downgrade in the 90-day window and the only sub-$200 target on the street.

post_earnings_tape_summary: At least six firms raised targets on April 30, 2026 alone (Goldman $275→$325, JPMorgan $280→$330, Morgan Stanley $300→$330, UBS $304→$333, Piper Sandler $260→$315, Susquehanna $300→$325). Truist followed on May 29. Zero post-Q1 downgrades. The DA Davidson February downgrade — the only negative in the 90-day window — was based on AWS market share loss that Q1 2026 data directly refuted. The post-Q1 tape is a coordinated wall of raises across bulge-bracket and mid-tier firms with no dissent on direction.

sentiment.short_interest_note: Under 1% of float; negligible short positioning; no meaningful short-side pressure; data as of April 2026 reporting period
sentiment.price_action: AMZN hit all-time high of $278.56 on May 5, 2026 (five trading days post-Q1 print); has since retraced 14.7% to $237.50 by June 18, 2026; stock is below 50-day MA ($256.64) but above 200-day MA ($232.71)

street_reconstruction.implied_revenue_cagr_note: FY2025 revenue $716.9bn to implied ~$1,200bn by FY2030 = ~10.8% CAGR; FY2026E $795.6bn to $1,200bn by FY2030 = ~10.8% from that base. Consistent across entry points.
street_reconstruction.implied_op_margin_note: Q1 2026 operating margin was 13.1% (record); street consensus for FY2026 targets ~14% EBIT margin on $795.6bn revenue (~$111bn EBIT); $313 target requires further expansion toward 15-16% by FY2028-2030 as AWS mix rises
street_reconstruction.implied_wacc_note: CAPM at beta 1.44, 4.2% risk-free rate, 4.5-5% ERP implies WACC of 10.7-11.4%; a $313 target implies the discount rate applied is at the lower end (~9-10%), which is consistent with large-cap quality pricing but generous given the capex risk
street_reconstruction.implied_exit_pe_note: JPMorgan's explicit post-earnings methodology: 32x FY2027 EPS of $10.37 = $331; consensus range implies ~28-32x FY2027 GAAP EPS
street_reconstruction.street_thesis_one_line: The $313 consensus requires ~11% annual revenue growth through FY2030, EBIT margins expanding to ~14-16% as AWS (37.7% margin, 28% growth, $364bn backlog) becomes a larger revenue share, and a 28-32x exit multiple on FY2027 earnings — a thesis entirely contingent on the $200bn capex cycle converting into sustained AWS share gain and high-margin cloud revenue rather than structurally impairing FCF

estimate_artifact_check.contamination_mechanism: Q1 2026 net income of $30.3bn includes a $16.8bn pre-tax non-cash Anthropic equity mark-to-market gain (ASC 321) in non-operating income. yfinance's NTM EPS computation rolls this non-recurring gain into the forward estimate pool, artificially inflating the NTM figure from the true operating ~$7.75 to ~$9.86. The resulting pe_ntm of 24.1x meaningfully understates the true operating P/E.
estimate_artifact_check.corrected_eps_source: Consensus cluster: SimplyWallSt $6.72 (post-revision low), TIKR $7.72 normalized, FinBox/WallStreetZen range $7.49-$8.21, JPMorgan-implied FY2026 operating basis; midpoint $7.75 used as corrected NTM proxy. Range $6.72-$8.21 reflects genuine analyst disagreement on capex impact, not a data error.
estimate_artifact_check.corrected_pe_note: At corrected $7.75 NTM EPS and $237.50 price, true forward P/E is ~30.6x — consistent with the TTM PE of 30.6x and reflecting normal continuity. The bundle pe_ntm of 24.1x is an artifact, not a re-rating.
estimate_artifact_check.gaap_vs_nongaap_gap: Material — Q1 2026 headline EPS $2.78 vs operating EPS ~$1.55 (ex-Anthropic gain); full-year impact is uncertain as future Anthropic mark-to-market gains/losses will be volatile
estimate_artifact_check.one_time_items: Q1 2026: $16.8bn pre-tax Anthropic investment gain (non-cash, non-recurring by nature); SBC FY2025: $19.5bn (2.7% of revenue, trending down from $22.0bn in FY2024)
estimate_artifact_check.capex_trend: Sharply accelerating — FY2024: ~$82.7bn, FY2025: $131.8bn (+59%), FY2026 guided: ~$200bn (+52%); FCF compressed to $7.7bn in FY2025; P/FCF not a useful current anchor
estimate_artifact_check.fcf_recovery_thesis: Bulls argue FCF will normalize as the capex cycle matures; if capex stabilizes near $150bn by FY2028 and CFO grows to ~$180-200bn, implied FCF of ~$30-50bn yields a normalized P/FCF of ~50-85x — still premium but within range of historical AWS-growth comps

capex_guidance.quote: Amazon expects approximately $200 billion in full-year 2026 capital expenditures, primarily for AWS and generative AI data center infrastructure — up ~52% from $131.8bn in FY2025; Q1 2026 capex was $44.2bn

The four hyperscalers collectively guided toward ~$690-700 billion in 2026 capital expenditures for AI data center buildouts — a ~62% increase over combined 2025 spending of ~$388bn; approximately 75% (~$515bn) is directly AI infrastructure

Anthropic committed $100bn+ to AWS compute over approximately 10 years (on top of a prior $38bn contract); this is demand-locked pipeline not included in the Q1 2026 $364bn RPO figure; Amazon CEO Andy Jassy cited it as evidence the backlog 'has reasonable breadth'

market_view.whats_priced_in: At $237.50 — 14.7% below the May 5 all-time high of $278.56 and 31.8% below the $313 consensus target — the market is pricing sustained AWS growth above 25% and EBIT margin expansion toward 14-16% as AWS becomes a larger revenue mix. The $200bn capex cycle is being treated as demand-driven investment that converts to AWS revenue acceleration, not a structural FCF drain. The corrected forward P/E of ~30.6x requires this thesis to be right; any sustained AWS deceleration or capex ROI disappointment would produce a significant de-rating.
market_view.street_view: The street's central argument is that AWS has re-accelerated to 28% (fastest in 15 quarters) and the $364bn RPO backlog — up 93% year-over-year, up 50% quarter-over-quarter — constitutes the clearest contracted demand signal in cloud history. Advertising at $17.2bn (+24%) provides a second high-margin compounding engine. The $200bn capex commitment is read by the bulls as demand-driven, not speculative: the backlog already covers a large fraction of the implied revenue. Analysts with the highest targets argue that Trainium custom silicon creates structural cost advantages that allow AWS to win AI workloads at lower cost than third-party GPU instances.
market_view.post_earnings_analyst_actions: Wall Street's response to Q1 2026 results was a coordinated wall of raises: Goldman Sachs $275→$325 (April 30), JPMorgan $280→$330 (April 30), Morgan Stanley $300→$330 (April 30), UBS $304→$333 (April 30), Piper Sandler $260→$315 (April 30), Susquehanna $300→$325 (April 30), Truist $320 reiteration (May 29). Zero post-Q1 downgrades or target cuts. The sole bear voice — DA Davidson's February 6 downgrade to Neutral / $175 on AWS market share concerns — was predicated on AWS growth lagging Azure and GCP; Q1's 28% reacceleration directly refuted that thesis. The post-Q1 tape is a hard bullish market fact.
market_view.news_narrative: Q1 2026 was a beat-and-raise quarter. Amazon beat on every line — revenue $181.5bn vs. $177.3bn expected, OI $23.9bn vs. ~$20.7bn expected (15% beat), AWS 28% growth vs. 25% consensus — AND raised Q2 guidance to $194-199bn revenue and $20-24bn OI, both well above the prior consensus. The headline EPS of $2.78 vs. $1.65 expected was substantially driven by the $16.8bn Anthropic non-operating gain; the genuine operating beat was in OI and AWS growth. The stock hit an all-time high of $278.56 on May 5, 2026, then retraced 14.7% to $237.50 by June 18 — the pullback reflects investor concern over $44.2bn Q1 capex and near-term FCF compression, not a change in the fundamental thesis.
market_view.sentiment_narrative: Sentiment is strongly bullish with minimal contradiction. 94% buy / 6% hold / 0% sell among 63 analysts; short interest at 0.88% of float (84.8M shares, 1.86 days to cover) is negligible. The stock trades above its 200-day moving average ($232.71) but below the 50-day ($256.64), consistent with a correction-within-uptrend pattern. The primary bear narrative is a valuation argument — at ~30.6x corrected NTM EPS and with $200bn capex compressing near-term FCF — not a fundamental one. The consensus implies 31.8% upside from current price.
market_view.runway: The fundamental demand runway is documented and contracted: $364bn AWS backlog (+93% YoY) converts to revenue over a 2-3 year horizon; the additional $100bn+ Anthropic commitment is outside the RPO. Advertising at $70bn+ annualized (+24%) is a second high-margin compounder. North America retail margins expanded from 7% to 9% in one year, demonstrating ongoing efficiency. The risk to the runway is not demand (the backlog answers that question) but execution: whether $200bn of annual capex converts to sufficient incremental AWS capacity and whether AWS margin holds as Azure and GCP compete.
market_view.runway_verdict: Fundamental demand runway is strong and multi-year, backed by contracted commitments. Valuation risk is separate and real: at ~30.6x corrected NTM earnings, the stock prices near-flawless execution of the margin expansion story. Multiple compression if capex ROI disappoints or AWS growth decelerates below 20% is a distinct risk from the demand story and must be reflected in the valuation range.
recent_news: Q1 2026 earnings (April 29): Revenue $181.5bn (+17%), AWS $37.6bn (+28%, 15-quarter high), OI $23.9bn (13.1% margin, record); $16.8bn Anthropic non-operating gain inflated headline EPS to $2.78
AWS backlog hits $364bn at Q1 end (+93% YoY, +50% QoQ); excludes separate Anthropic $100bn+ commitment
Q2 2026 guidance: revenue $194-199bn, OI $20-24bn — both above prior consensus; includes Prime Day in Q2
FY2026 capex guided at ~$200bn for AI data center infrastructure; Q1 capex $44.2bn
Six major banks raise price targets April 30, 2026: Goldman $275→$325, JPMorgan $280→$330, Morgan Stanley $300→$330 among the moves; zero cuts
AMZN hit all-time high $278.56 on May 5, 2026; retraced 14.7% to $237.50 by June 18, 2026
DA Davidson downgraded to Neutral / $175 on February 6, 2026 citing AWS market share loss; thesis refuted by Q1 28% growth reacceleration
Anthropic committed $100bn+ to AWS on top of an existing $38bn contract — announced alongside Q1 results
market_view.market_lean_rationale: 94% buy consensus, zero active sell ratings, $364bn contracted backlog, coordinated wall of post-Q1 target raises, negligible short interest. The only structural bear argument (DA Davidson) was refuted by Q1 data. The lean is strong bull on the fundamental runway; the moderating factor is valuation — the stock prices an execution outcome, not a lottery, and the corrected ~30.6x forward P/E leaves limited margin for error on the capex ROI thesis.
